title: Kannimara Teak
tagline: Kannimara Teak — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: The Kannimara Teak is one of the world's biggest living teak (Tectona grandis) trees.
overview.description: The Kannimara Teak is one of the world's biggest living teak (Tectona grandis) trees. It is found at Parambikulam Tiger Reserve in Palakkad district, Kerala. It stands at a height of 41.83 meters and has a girth of 7.24 meters (measured at breast height), with an estimated age of approximately 500 years. In recognition of its significance, the Government of India awarded the tree the Mahavriksha Puraskar (Great Tree Award) in 1994.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Peruvaripallam Dam
topPlaces.description: Peruvaripallam Dam is an earth-filled embankment dam on the Peruvaripallam River in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. The dam's reservoir is connected to the nearby Thunacadavu Reservoir to the south by an open cut channel.

topPlaces.name: Thunakkadavu Dam
topPlaces.description: Thunakkadavu Dam is situated in Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, Kerala across Thoonakkadavu River, which is a tributary of Parambikulam River, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. It is part of the Parambikulam-Aliyar Irrigation Project.

topPlaces.name: Parambikulam Dam
topPlaces.description: Parambikulam Dam is an embankment dam on the Parambikulam River, located in the Palakkad district in the Western Ghats of Kerala, India, ranks number one in India as well as in the top ten embankment dams in the world in volume in the year 2000. This Dam was built at the time of Kamarajar.

topPlaces.name: Parambikulam Tiger Reserve
topPlaces.description: Parambikulam Tiger Reserve, which also includes the erstwhile Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, is a 643.66 square kilometres (248.5 mi2) protected area lying in Palakkad district and Thrissur district of Kerala state, South India. The Wildlife Sanctuary, which had an area of 285 square kilometres (110 mi2) was established in part in 1973 and 1984.

topPlaces.name: Karimala Gopuram
topPlaces.description: Karimala Gopuram (കരിമല ഗോപുരം) is the highest peak in the Thrissur district of Kerala. It is located at southern boundary of Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Padagiri
topPlaces.description: Padagiri, also known as Nellikota, is a hill in the Nelliyampathy Hills, in Chittur Taluk, Palakkad District, Kerala, and is the range's tallest peak, with an elevation of 1585m. It is around six kilometres from Nelliampathy.

items.note: Peruvaripallam Dam is an earth-filled embankment dam on the Peruvaripallam River in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. The dam's reservoir is connected to the nearby Thunacadavu Reservoir to the south by an open cut channel.

Thunakkadavu Dam is situated in Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, Kerala across Thoonakkadavu River, which is a tributary of Parambikulam River, in Palakkad district of Kerala, India. It is part of the Parambikulam-Aliyar Irrigation Project.

Parambikulam Dam is an embankment dam on the Parambikulam River, located in the Palakkad district in the Western Ghats of Kerala, India, ranks number one in India as well as in the top ten embankment dams in the world in volume in the year 2000. This Dam was built at the time of Kamarajar.

Parambikulam Tiger Reserve, which also includes the erstwhile Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary, is a 643.66 square kilometres (248.5 mi2) protected area lying in Palakkad district and Thrissur district of Kerala state, South India. The Wildlife Sanctuary, which had an area of 285 square kilometres (110 mi2) was established in part in 1973 and 1984.
Karimala Gopuram (കരിമല ഗോപുരം) is the highest peak in the Thrissur district of Kerala. It is located at southern boundary of Parambikulam Wildlife Sanctuary.

Padagiri, also known as Nellikota, is a hill in the Nelliyampathy Hills, in Chittur Taluk, Palakkad District, Kerala, and is the range's tallest peak, with an elevation of 1585m. It is around six kilometres from Nelliampathy.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kannimara Teak?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kannimara Teak is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kannimara Teak?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kannimara Teak?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kannimara Teak at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kannimara Teak?
faq.a: Stays in Kannimara Teak start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kannimara Teak?
faq.a: The top attractions are Peruvaripallam Dam, Thunakkadavu Dam, Parambikulam Dam, Parambikulam Tiger Reserve, Karimala Gopuram.
